published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovCinelliNeweySharmaSyrgkanis2022: sharp Riesz omitted-variable-bias support bound for broad linear causal functionals, including policy effects

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 is closest but targets joint propensity/outcome-misspecification value intervals, not CH-Riesz minimax regret

published_prior_art.cite: HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2026 and JinXuZhengZhou2025: sharp/robust policy learning under MSM; they do not state a CH-Riesz Tikhonov rule

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 has a compatibility warning for finite-grid joint misspecification, not this arm-indexed CH-Riesz iff frontier

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup / Assumption ch-budget / Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: As defined, the Riesz representer of L_h,Q in the e0-weighted arm tangent space is exactly a_h^0 for every Q, so eta_R is identically zero and the positive-budget CH-Riesz covariance frontier collapses.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Conjecture 1 claims sharp worst-case regret J under the standing assumptions, but sharpness is only characterized later in Conjecture 2 by an additional P-compatible collinearity condition; without that condition Proposition 1 is only an upper bound.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2 bridge limits
soundness_flags.one_line: Lambda_CH is not defined well enough for the Kallus-Zhou bridge: MSM support functions generally depend on the full distribution of W_h, not only its variance, so a single h-independent calibration map need not make the stated limit meaningful over B-B.

soundness_flags.label: Proof-sketch route
soundness_flags.one_line: The proof sketch says to show CH-Riesz support converges to the Kallus-Zhou odds-ratio support, but names no concrete MSM support formula or distributional condition under which a variance-only CH support can equal the odds-ratio support.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the latent Riesz increment using the actual latent assignment/observation operator, likely with e_Q(a|X,U) or the observed-data projection operator, so eta_R is nonzero and the CH covariance term is not vacuous.
Split Conjecture 1 into an upper-bound minimizer for the ellipsoidal support envelope and a sharp-regret minimizer conditional on the Conjecture 2 compatibility frontier.

Replace the Kallus-Zhou bridge normalization with an explicit support-function equation: specify whether Lambda_CH is contrast-specific, distribution-class-specific, or valid only under a named elliptical/Gaussian score family.
Add a boundary corollary for d=1 or for a fixed standardized score distribution where the CH support and MSM support can be compared algebraically without hiding the calibration in an assumption.

recommended_next_step: Patch the latent Riesz geometry first; until eta_R is non-vacuous and the MSM bridge map is explicit, the novelty kernel is not well-posed enough for acceptance.
